Velarix
DocsGitHubLogin
Paper / Ink

Causal memory for AI agents

Memory you can correct.

Velarix gives agents a causal memory layer. When a fact changes, stale reasoning is pruned instead of quietly surviving in context.

The point is not more interface. The point is less ambiguity in the runtime your agents already use.

Get API keyRead docs

Why it exists

Models are good at continuation. Velarix is the layer that notices when the premise changed and removes the downstream fiction.

Integration

One import swap

Keep your existing OpenAI workflow and add a session id when memory needs to persist.

Correction

Prune stale chains

A changed premise can invalidate the reasoning built on top of it instead of silently lingering.

Deployment

Self-host or cloud

Run it locally for free or move to managed sessions when you need shared state and billing.

Import swap

The integration stays familiar. Replace the import, pass a session id, keep the rest of your client flow.

runtime.py
Standard clientStateless request flow
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI()

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Execute..."}],
)
With VelarixCausal memory attached
from velarix.adapters.openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(velarix_session_id="research-1")

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Execute..."}],
)

What changes

Correct stale reasoning

Velarix tracks causal dependencies, so one updated fact can invalidate the conclusions that depended on it.

Show the why-trace

Every decision can be traced back through the beliefs that produced it, which makes debugging much less theatrical.

Stay close to the runtime

The interface is intentionally small. You do not have to adopt a new orchestration layer just to keep memory honest.

forever

Velarix

Minimal surface area. Correctable runtime state. Fewer places for bad assumptions to hide.
